channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: How Insurers Are Using AI to Manage Rising Catastrophic and Accumulation Risk
item.description: In the U.S., 2025 was the sixth consecutive year in which total catastrophic losses topped $100 billion , and in the UK, insurers paid out a record £6.1 billion in property claims in 2025, with an estimated £1.2 billion of those claims coming from adverse weather events, following catastrophic rainfall and flooding throughout the country. The insurance and reinsurance industry is facing a sustained increase in catastrophic and location‑based risks from extreme weather and natural catastrophes to political violence, terrorism and complex industrial losses.

item.title: PartnerRe hands APAC trio new leadership roles, with regional CEO James Beedle to retire
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA publishes findings from financial adviser market survey
item.description: We have published findings from our Financial Adviser Survey . The findings provide an updated picture of how the UK financial advice market is evolving and what this means for firms, consumers and future growth. The survey brings together responses from more than 4,100 financial advice firms; alongside analysis of data we already hold on around 31,000 advisers.

item.title: FCA calls on law firms and claims management companies to consider the position of their clients
item.description: Any law firm or claims management company (CMC) involved in a potential challenge against the scheme that also has clients making motor finance claims should consider their position and that of their clients carefully. They should give those clients the option of exiting the contract and strongly consider waiving any fees. At the very least, they should write to those clients to explain they’re involved in a challenge that’s likely to delay compensation.

item.title: Sapia agrees to pay more than £19m to WealthTek clients after failing to protect client money
item.description: This resulted in Sapia holding and being responsible for protecting client money resulting from WealthTek’s activities. Sapia has agreed to make a voluntary payment of £19,637,950 to WealthTek clients and the FCA has censured the firm. The FCA found Sapia did not put enough safeguards in place to protect this money.

item.title: Sector Snapshot: EV Funding On Track For Modest Gains
item.description: The broad trend: Funding to EV-related startups reflects a similar mix of optimism and restraint. Yet funding remains far below prior peaks and exit activity appears muted. The numbers: Companies in Crunchbase’s electric vehicle category are on track to see higher investment this year relative to last.

item.title: Exclusive: Cloneable Raises $4.6M To ‘Clone’ Expert Worker Knowledge With Agentic AI For Utilities And Infrastructure
item.description: If we know what the expert is looking for, why can’t we just clone that expertise?” The startup’s founders realized that heavy industries — energy, oil and gas and agriculture — face a “knowledge crisis” as experienced workers retire faster than they can be replaced. Cloneable aims to capture and preserve that kind of institutional knowledge. Cloneable , a startup that uses AI to shadow human experts in heavy industries such as energy and replicate their specialized workflows into autonomous agents, has raised $4.6 million in seed funding, the company tells Crunchbase News exclusively.

item.title: Euler ILS Partners sees $1bn fund opportunity for data centre sidecar risk: Bloomberg
item.description: Euler ILS Partners, the specialist Swiss insurance-linked securities (ILS) investment manager, sees a $1 billion fund opportunity based on quota share’s to support insurance risk transfer capacity for the data centre build-out, according to reporting from Bloomberg. The publisher has interviewed Niklaus Hilti, Chief Investment Officer of Euler ILS Partners and said the company is targeting launch of a fund dedicated to assuming risk from data centres, while the goal will be to deliver double-digit returns to investors. Hilti told Bloomberg that Euler ILS Partners is planning to partner with an insurer to underwrite the specialist policies covering elements of risk related to data centres.

item.title: Regional hurricane landfall patterns, not basin-wide totals, key for 2026 ILS portfolios: Hohermuth, Schroders Capital
item.description: Benjamin Hohermuth of Schroders Capital ILS suggests that for insurance-linked securities (ILS) portfolios, the regional message is far more important than basin-wide totals. In a new article authored by Hohermuth, who serves as a Senior Nat Cat Specialist, Non-Life at Schroders Capital ILS, the specialist shared the firm’s outlook for the insurance-linked securities market as the key 2026 Atlantic hurricane season approaches. “For an ILS portfolio, the regional landfall pattern matters more than basin-wide landfalls.

item.title: Global property insurance rates fall 9% in Q1 2026, as appetites broaden: Marsh Risk
item.description: Broker Marsh Risk has revealed that property rates declined by 9% globally in the opening quarter of 2026, repeating the same trend seen in Q4 2025, with double-digit decreases recorded in major regions such as the Pacific, the United States and the United Kingdom. In its latest report on global commercial insurance rates, Marsh Risk highlighted that the downward rate movement in Q1’26 was supported by favourable reinsurance terms and a significant capacity oversupply. Commenting on the report, John Donnelly, President, Global Placement, Marsh Risk, said: “While the Middle East conflict is being carefully observed for its potential impact on insurance markets, the current competitive environment is expected to persist as insurer profitability remains strong.

item.title: Kingstone Companies extends Golden’s term as President and CEO till January, 2029
item.description: The Board of Directors of property and casualty (P&C) insurance holding company, Kingstone Companies, Inc., has extended Meryl Golden’s term as President and Chief Executive Officer through January 10th, 2029. Thomas Newgarden, Chairman of the Board, Kingstone Companies, commented, “This extension follows a review by the Compensation Committee and reflects the Board’s confidence in Ms Golden and in the plan she is executing. The Compensation Committee recommended this extension, as Golden’s current agreement was set to expire on January 10th, 2027.

item.title: Cyber insurance market enters critical phase amid softening rates and rising exposure: DUAL
item.description: Richard Clapham, CEO of DUAL, has warned that the global cyber insurance market is approaching a “significant inflection point”, as sustained softening, rising exposures and an increasingly elevated threat landscape converge to test long-term market sustainability. In a recent report, DUAL said cyber insurance has reached a critical juncture. “Cyber insurance has moved rapidly through distinct phases: early exposure-led growth, a sharp pricing correction and, more recently, sustained softening.

item.title: BMA and Lloyd’s introduce streamlined Market Entry pathway for insurance innovation
item.description: The Bermuda Monetary Authority (BMA), the regulator responsible for Bermuda’s financial services sector including insurance supervision, has introduced a new Market Entry & Innovation Pathway intended to support firms with established solutions in progressing more efficiently into the Bermuda market. The pathway is open to companies that have participated in, or graduated from, the Lloyd’s Lab programme and whose solutions are relevant to Bermuda’s insurance environment. Gerald Gakundi, Head of Insurance and Funds and Deputy Managing Director at the BMA, commented: “This streamlined process strengthens Bermuda’s position as a jurisdiction where innovation can thrive by combining regulatory clarity with global connectivity.

item.title: Beazley Shareholders Approve Zurich’s $10.9 Billion Cash Takeover
item.description: London-listed specialty insurer Beazley said on Wednesday shareholders approved Swiss group Zurich Insurance’s 8.1 billion pound ($10.94 billion) all-cash takeover, with 99.9% votes in favor at a meeting held earlier in the day. Earlier in March, Zurich raised 3.9 billion Swiss francs ($4.98 billion) in a share sale to help finance the Beazley acquisition , after the British insurer accepted an improved offer of up to 1,335 pence per share. Days after Zurich struck the deal with Beazley, it also agreed to buy Generali’s Irish P&C operations for 337 million euros ($394.69 million).

item.title: PwC Pays $166 Million to Settle HK Evergrande Audit Probe
item.description: PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP agreed to pay HK$1.3 billion ($166 million) in fines and compensation to settle investigations into its auditing work for the collapsed property giant China Evergrande Group. The measures come as the firm attempts to rebuild in the wake of Beijing’s earlier record fine over its audit work on China Evergrande. PwC was Evergrande’s auditor for more than a decade until it resigned in January 2023, due to what the developer said were audit-related disagreements.

item.title: Ex-Credit Suisse Team Plans $1 Billion Fund for Data Center Insurance Risk
item.description: A team of former Credit Suisse bankers is preparing to launch a fund they say will offer double-digit returns in exchange for taking on some of the insurance risk tied to data centers. It would be the first time a product in the market for insurance-linked securities specifically targets data-center risk, according to Hilti. Euler ILS Partners , an alternative investor formed through a 2024 management buyout of Credit Suisse Insurance Linked Strategies Ltd., is in the process of teaming up with an insurance company to underwrite specialist policies, Chief Investment Officer Niklaus Hilti told Bloomberg.

item.title: Simply Business has launched a new business insurance app within ChatGPT
item.description: Simply Business has launched a new business insurance app within ChatGPT in the U.S., alongside a separate rollout of a UK version, marking a further step in its digital distribution strategy. By embedding pricing capabilities into ChatGPT, Simply Business is aiming to simplify early-stage insurance discovery while maintaining a secure and structured path to full policy purchase through its platform. and the UK for small businesses in ChatGPT is our latest step in meeting our customers where they are and making the insurance-buying process an easier, better and fairer experience for them.” Dana Edwards, Group CTO at Simply Business, added: “Small business owners are already using platforms like ChatGPT to research, plan and make decisions.

item.title: Berkley errs on the side of being cautious and cheap
item.description: But as we’ve shared, we tend to err on the side of being cautious and cheap. Berkley reported a record quarter with operating EPS of $1.30, beating expectations by 14%, alongside a 21.2% ROE and $515 million in net income. But again, you never know what tomorrow will bring, but there’s a reason why we have not been historically active on that front.” Bottom Line: They dont need Berkley One, they need One Berkley.

item.title: Simply Business launches ChatGPT app
item.description: Simply Business launched a business insurance app in ChatGPT in the U.S., with a separate version for the UK. and the UK for small businesses in ChatGPT is our latest step in meeting our customers where they are and making the insurance-buying process an easier, better and fairer experience for them.” – David Summers, Group CEO at Simply Business. “Small business owners are already using platforms like ChatGPT to research, plan and make decisions.

item.title: Neptune leans into AI to create “super agents”
item.description: The company’s goal is clear: turn traditional agents into “super agents” capable of selling more efficiently and consistently. “Last quarter, I spoke about turning agents into what we call super agents. Neptune Flood is leaning into AI across its platform, aiming to reshape how flood insurance is sold while expanding its reach in an underinsured market.

item.title: Jochen Wilmes se une a Miller Spain para liderar el área de riesgos financieros
item.description: Miller Spain ha reforzado su posicionamiento en el segmento de grandes cuentas y riesgos complejos con la incorporación de Jochen Wilmes como director de Caución, Crédito y Riesgos. Miller Spain enmarca este nombramiento en su plan estratégico de crecimiento , que incluye el refuerzo de equipos y la especialización, con la incorporación reciente de Dina Urbano al área de Crédito y Caución y el apoyo de María Jesús Lainez y el resto del equipo. Con una sólida trayectoria en el ámbito asegurador y financiero, está especializado en la implementación de soluciones de riesgo y en la estructuración de programas de gestión para compañías nacionales e internacionales.

item.title: Santalucía crea la Dirección General de Desarrollo Corporativo y Estrategia
item.description: Santalucía incorpora a Alfonso Martínez como director general de Desarrollo Corporativo y Estrategia, una posición que integra las actuales direcciones corporativas de ambas áreas y que pasa a formar parte del Comité Ejecutivo de la compañía. Desde esta posición, Martínez será el responsable de definir, liderar y coordinar la estrategia corporativa global, además de impulsar las iniciativas de desarrollo corporativo. La creación de esta nueva Dirección General se realiza dentro del marco establecido en el Plan Estratégico ‘Contigo2028’ , en una apuesta por el crecimiento orgánico e inorgánico de la entidad.

item.title: IA en mediación: cuando la tecnología eleva el listón
item.description: Y ese cambio no diluye el papel del mediador humano, sino que eleva el listón de calidad al que debe responder. Sin embargo, en la mediación, lo que seguramente cambie el paradigma actual no es tanto la utilización de la IA por parte de los mediadores, sino la adopción de la inteligencia artificial por parte de los clientes . La mediación tiene dos misiones fundamentales: acercar el seguro a los potenciales clientes y asistir durante toda la vida del seguro.

item.title: Nationwide: Drivers Feel Roads Are Less Predictable Amid Distracted and Aggressive Behavior
item.description: According to Nationwide, the findings reflect a road environment marked by rising tension and less predictable behavior, increasing the need for proactive risk guidance from risk professionals. Aggressive and distracted driving is increasingly being reported by motorists across the United States, creating a road environment that makes many feel is harder to predict, according to new research. Compared with Nationwide’s 2025 findings, distraction among company drivers rose from 67% to 77%, recklessness increased from 63% to 73% and reports of unsafe behavior around larger vehicles climbed from 59% to 74%.

item.title: US Labor Department Unveils Proposal on Contract, Franchise Worker Pay
item.description: Department of Labor unveiled a proposed rule that would narrow the circumstances in which a business can be considered the “joint employer” of another company’s workers under the federal law requiring a minimum wage and overtime pay. Acting Labor Secretary Keith Sonderling said the goal of the proposal was to make it easier for employers to comply with the law, ultimately benefiting workers. The proposal will be formally published on Thursday and the department will collect public comments for 60 days.

item.title: Ford Recalling 140,000 US Vehicles Over Damaged Wires
item.description: Related: Ford Recalling 1.4 Million Pickup Trucks Over Gearshift Issue Dealers will inspect the wiring for damage and update the body control module software, NHTSA said, adding that the damaged wiring harnesses will be replaced as necessary. as damaged wires can create an electrical short and cause a fire in the A-pillar area, increasing the risk of injury or a crash, the U.S. The sun visor or headliner wiring harnesses may be improperly positioned or have excessive tape thickness, which can result in damaged wires, the regulator said.

item.title: Australia’s PSC Insurance Brokers appoints James Thornberry as head of trade credit and surety
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Seconded employees of Japanese insurers allegedly took Toyota data without permission: report
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Hong Kong ‘ideally placed’ to promote use of alternative risk transfer and ILS: IA’s Stephen Yiu
item.description: Given Hong Kong’s open and sophisticated financial market, along with its distinct connection with the Chinese Mainland and the support of roughly 3,380 family offices, the major city is ideally placed to promote the usage of alternative risk transfer tools and ILS, according to Stephen Yiu, Chairman of the Hong Kong Insurance Authority (IA). “Gifted with an open and sophisticated financial market, unique connectivity with the Chinese Mainland and the patronage of some 3,380 family offices, Hong Kong is ideally placed to promote the usage of alternative risk transfer tools.” Yiu continued: “Our journey of becoming an ILS hub began in 2021 with the setting up of a bespoke regulatory regime and launching of the pilot grant scheme. “Alternative investments are gaining more attention than ever from institutional investors looking for balanced risk-adjusted return amidst rising uncertainties, as borne out by the massive flow of capital into ILS products that propelled the outstanding amount to a historical level in the first quarter of 2026, with total assets under management surpassing US$129 billion,” he said.

item.title: Spinnaker Insurance reaches AM Best FSC IX amid continued capital markets activity
item.description: Spinnaker Insurance Company, a wholly owned subsidiary of Hippo Holdings that provides property and casualty solutions to the program insurance market, has reported that it has reached an AM Best Financial Size Category (FSC) of IX, representing policyholder surplus above $250 million. Spinnaker Insurance Company indicates that the upgrade reflects ongoing business expansion, solid capital positioning, and a consistent approach to execution within the program insurance sector. Spinnaker Insurance Company, together with its affiliates, operates nationwide as a property and casualty insurer and holds an A- rating from AM Best.

item.title: Kristyn Smallcombe named CUO of Ascot U.S.
item.description: Ascot, a global specialty insurance and reinsurance group, has announced the appointment of Kristyn Smallcombe as Chief Underwriting Officer (CUO), Ascot U.S. In her new role, Smallcombe will be responsible for shaping and executing Ascot’s U.S. She will report to Matt Kramer, CEO of Ascot U.S., and partner closely with the U.S.

item.title: Openly expands reinsurance partnership with Allianz Re alongside new growth investment
item.description: Openly, a technology-enabled homeowners insurance provider, has announced an expansion of its long-term reinsurance partnership with Allianz Re, strengthening the structure that supports its homeowners insurance portfolio. Openly notes that the additional investment capital, combined with the strengthened reinsurance partnership, provides resources to support its ongoing expansion strategy. This strengthened partnership with Allianz Re, alongside continued support from our investors, accelerates that commitment: more states, more products, and a stronger platform for every agent who chooses to build their book with us.” “Openly is revolutionising homeowners insurance with a disciplined, scalable underwriting model that emphasizes rigorous risk selection, precise pricing, and consistent portfolio management—yielding strong loss ratio results despite high natural catastrophe activity in the US in recent years,” added Niklas Mundorf, Senior Manager at Allianz X North America.

item.title: People Moves: Goldie to Oversee Nationwide’s Management Liability, Cyber Teams; Former FIO & NAIC Exec. Joins The Council; Smallcombe Appointed CUO, Ascot US
item.description: Bobbie Goldie to Oversee Nationwide’s Private Company Management Liability, Cyber Liability Teams Nationwide is tapping insurance industry veteran Bobbie Goldie to oversee the insurance and financial services company’s private company management liability and cyber liability teams. She’ll assume that role on May 4 and report to Tom Iorio, Nationwide’s senior vice president of management liability and specialty. Joins The Council of Insurance Agents & Brokers Brooke Stringer has joined The Council as Vice President of Government Affairs , bringing with her a breadth of experience developing policy strategies for congressional, executive branch, state government, and industry stakeholders.

item.title: Viewpoint: Middle Market Businesses Are Cutting Costs – But May Be Increasing Risks
item.description: Economic pressure is also prompting many businesses to re-evaluate their insurance policies, and many middle market owners are exploring strategies to manage premium costs – including renegotiating policies, increasing deductibles or reducing coverages and limits. When I talk with our agents that serve middle market clients, the same themes consistently emerge: middle market businesses are optimistic, but they’re also fatigued and looking for more than traditional risk transfer from their insurance partners. Workforce strain and investment are rising together Middle market businesses report a range of financial concerns, led by inflation, high interest rates, tariffs, the potential for an economic downturn and supply chain disruptions.

item.title: EIP Nexacore Form Southeast Asia Partnership
item.description: By combining Nexacore’s reach in Southeast Asia with EIP’s technology and expertise, we’re creating a practical route for clients in the region to launch robust device protection and warranty programs at scale.” Nexacore says the partnership expands its capabilities by integrating embedded insurance technology with its administrative services. EIP (London) has partnered with Nexacore (Singapore) to deliver device protection and extended warranty solutions across Southeast Asia. “Southeast Asia represents a major growth opportunity for device protection and extended warranty but insurers and retailers are often still forced to stitch together multiple providers across technology, administration and local delivery,” says Ross Sinclair, Founder and CEO, EIP.

item.title: Legal Analysis: Insurer Subrogation Rights Under Scrutiny
item.description: Two recent court decisions have addressed the issue of subrogation rights of insurance companies against upstream or downstream vendors that experience a data security breach: Axis Insurance Company v. Zoll settled those claims with its customers and then brought an arbitration claim against Fusion, with which it was in privity, and a subrogation claim against Barracuda, which lacked privity. As part of their arbitration and settlements, Zoll’s and Fusion’s claims against Barracuda were assigned to Axis, Fusion’s insurer.

item.title: Hong Kong advances ILS frameworks, including sidecars, Bermuda ties: IA chief
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Allianz, Jio sign binding deal for 50:50 India insurance JV
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: IAG NZ reports over 800 claims from heavy rain, flash flooding in Wellington
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Chubb CEO zeroes in on AI shift as “agentics” begins to take shape
item.description: On the company’s Q1 2026 earnings call, Greenberg said Chubb’s digital strategy remains unchanged, with execution on track despite a surge in new AI capabilities. Greenberg pointed to “agentics” and enterprise AI solutions as the most important emerging themes, though he noted the industry is still early in understanding how these tools will translate into real operations. The technology is evolving at a rapid pace… what’s still emerging is how agentics actually operationalize.

item.title: MSI launches canine liability insurance nationwide
item.description: MSI has introduced a canine liability insurance product designed for landlords and property managers, aiming to reduce the friction around renting to pet owners. Property owners often limit or reject pets due to liability concerns, particularly around dog-related incidents. The pitch is straightforward: remove the liability barrier, and leasing becomes easier.

item.title: Ripe expands Manchester footprint with 150-seat office
item.description: Ripe Thinking has moved into a new office at Stockport Exchange, increasing capacity from 60 to 150 seats as it scales operations in Greater Manchester. “Greater Manchester has been integral to our growth from day one. At Ripe, we protect the hobbies, interests and passions that people really care about – whether that’s in their personal or professional lives.

item.title: Scientists Burn Homes to Figure out How to Best Protect Them in Wildfires
item.description: Add moving vegetation, mulch, wood fences and hot tubs with their highly flammable insultation several feet away and experts said you can protect houses from the increasing danger of wildfires on a warming planet. They have set fire to 13 houses because scientists need to burn to learn. It took less than three minutes for wind-whipped flames to go from licking the side of the house to shattering a window and working under the eaves to burn everything inside.

item.title: Damage Still Being Assessed After Wisconsin Storms, Tornadoes
item.description: The strong storms that stranded cars and caused structural damage to at least 75 homes in Wisconsin last Friday were caused by several tornadoes, according to a preliminary report by the National Weather Service. The NWS confirmed several tornadoes resulted in structural damage. The severe weather was triggered by a surface low-pressure system and cold front across the upper Midwest which drew unusually high moisture into the region, with dew points climbing into the lower 60s into central Wisconsin, the weather service said.

item.title: River Deltas Sinking Faster Than Sea Level Rise Pose Risk to Millions: Study
item.description: Many of the world’s largest river deltas are subsiding more quickly than global sea levels are rising, putting hundreds of millions of people at potential risk, according to a new study published in Nature . In 18 of the 40 deltas, this downward movement, known as subsidence, already exceeds local sea-level rise. The research offers the first detailed, high-resolution analysis of elevation loss across 40 river deltas around the world.

item.title: Lawmakers Gather to Talk About AI; Angst and Fears of ‘Destruction’ Follow
item.description: A congressional subcommittee on April 16 held a roundtable discussion on the potential of artificial intelligence, which took a turn toward the existential as each lawmaker aired their anxieties about the rapidly evolving technology. Yassamin Ansari, D-Ariz., raised concerns about the Trump administration’s use of AI in the war with Iran, the technology’s intensive energy usage and its potential effects on the climate. While members of Congress elsewhere debated other major topics — the scope of the federal government’s surveillance powers, the war with Iran and funding the Department of Homeland Security — the House Oversight Committee’s subcommittee roundtable on “Artificial Intelligence and American Power” brought executives of AI firms, academics and those implementing AI at major companies together with lawmakers.

item.title: Getting firms fit to run
item.description: Getting authorised is more like a marathon than it might seem. Speech by Sheree Howard at the APCC Spring Conference 2026. Speaker: Sheree Howard, executive director of Authorisations Event: APCC Spring Conference 2026, London Delivered: 22 April 2026 Note: This is the speech as drafted which may differ from the delivered version Reading time: 8 minutes This weekend, tens of thousands of runners will line up in Greenwich Park for the start of the London Marathon.

item.title: PERILS launches 2026 IED, showing $150tn in global nat cat exposures
item.description: Catastrophe data aggregator PERILS has released the 2026 edition of its Industry Exposure Database (IED), providing USD $150 trillion of markets sums insured exposed to natural perils for 21 different countries, with like-for-like exposures also rising 4.7% year-on-year at constant exchange rates. In total the PERILS IED 2026 contains 67 individual market portfolios. The IED contains markets sums insured exposed to natural perils for 21 countries, including Australia, Austria, Belgium, Canada, Denmark, France, Germany, Ireland, Indonesia, Italy, Japan, Luxembourg, the Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, the Philippines, Sweden, Switzerland, Thailand, Turkey, and the United Kingdom.

item.title: Supply-demand softening property at a pace “I’ll only describe as dumb” – Chubb CEO Greenberg
item.description: Speaking during the Chubb first-quarter earnings call today, CEO Evan Greenberg called out supply-demand factors related to capital in the insurance and reinsurance industry, as well as high levels of intermediation cost, as softening the property insurance market at a pace he called “dumb”. “In a number of important markets, property and financial lines pricing conditions are soft, with property pricing in those markets softening at a pace that, frankly, I’ll only describe as dumb,” he stated. On large account property risks, both admitted and excess and surplus lines (E&S), Greenberg said the pricing levels are judged to be inadequate, driving the pull-back by Chubb in Q1.

item.title: Evolving flood and drought patterns are reshaping global re/insurance risk: Aon
item.description: Broker Aon states that intensifying flood and drought patterns are reshaping risk profiles across global insurance and reinsurance markets, according to its 2026 Climate and Catastrophe Insight (CCI) report. Based on Aon’s Climate Risk Monitor, rainfall-driven (pluvial) flood risk in the United States could increase by roughly 12% under a medium-emissions scenario and around 19% under a high-emissions scenario by mid-century. Citing National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP) data, Aon noted that only 2.6% of residential properties in US counties received NFIP payouts for 2025 flood events that were covered by NFIP policies.
